# W15T5 Implementation Plan

## Task Goal
Integrate SIMD and parallel dispatch into the math semantic layer without changing math public APIs, then add module-level integration coverage.

## Related Design Documents
- `design/11-math.md` §6.3
- `design/11-math.md` §8.4
- `design/11-math.md` §8.5
- `design/11-math.md` §9.1
- `design/08-simd.md` §5.4a
- `design/09-parallel.md` §5.3
- `design/01-architecture.md` Decision 6

## Dependencies
- W15T2
- W15T3
- W15T4
- W13T4

## Files to Create/Modify
- `src/math/mod.rs`
- `src/math/binary.rs`
- `src/math/unary.rs`
- `src/math/comparison.rs`
- `tests/test_math.rs`

## Implementation Details

### Dispatch Rules
- `dispatch.rs` decides serial vs parallel.
- On the non-parallel path, `math` may delegate to the internal `simd` backend.
- On the parallel path, `math` may delegate to `parallel::par_zip_map()` for the documented supported subset.
- Unsupported operations, unsupported types, non-contiguous views, or failed admission conditions must fall back to the scalar semantic implementation.

### SIMD Integration Scope
- Stable required SIMD acceleration is only for arithmetic `add/sub/mul/div` on the supported contiguous aligned subset.
- Other math operations remain on documented fallback paths unless the SIMD design explicitly marks them supported.

### Parallel Integration Scope
- Large inputs may use the `parallel/` backend.
- Worker-local SIMD is allowed after the parallel path is already chosen.
- No second-level parallelism may be triggered from within workers.

### No Signature Changes
This task adds internal dispatch plumbing only. It does not change any public method signature defined in W15T2 through W15T4.

## Test Plan
- `test_add_simd_vs_scalar`
- `test_mul_simd_vs_scalar`
- `test_large_tensor_add_parallel`
- `test_high_rank_broadcast`
- `test_empty_tensor`
- Feature matrix tests for default, `simd`, `parallel`, and `simd,parallel`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Public math method signatures remain unchanged.
- Contiguous admitted arithmetic inputs can use the `simd` backend.
- Large admitted inputs can use the `parallel` backend.
- Non-admitted inputs preserve scalar semantics and error categories.
- Integration tests cover default and feature-enabled configurations.

## Development Workflow
1. Add cross-path consistency tests before wiring dispatch.
2. Integrate SIMD dispatch for arithmetic methods.
3. Integrate parallel dispatch for supported broadcast arithmetic paths.
4. Re-run the full math integration matrix across feature combinations.
